ops.text:
CHECK-BACK E33 PREP (correctly stopped; TEST untouched; ~$13 of the $30 cap spent on harness+baseline). Harness built, SANITY GATE PASSED (self-agreement 0.87 dev-100 / 0.861 dev-50 ≥ 0.85 — E33 clears the same cross-model gate E34 failed, on its seed-33 subset). Baseline v6 sonnet: dev-100 binary agreement vs auditor 0.76, over-flag reproduced (fp=22 vs fn=2, matching E30.3). COST BREACH: the ClaudeCliBackend `claude -p` injects ~58K framework tokens/call (judge prompt ~3K); full funnel projects $195 est / $136 actual vs $30 cap; test stage alone ~$41-61 actual. Same root cause as E34.

ORCHESTRATOR RULING (shared across E33/E34/E35): the plan mis-priced the claude-CLI judge by ~6x. Three resolutions — (A) run faithful/scaffolded at true cost (~$400 for the three prompt lanes: eats the envelope, not viable), (B) go lean + CERTIFY the lean invocation reproduces the scaffolded judge's verdicts (if so, all three run cheap AND transfer to the deployed judge), (C) descope (weakens the science). Pursuing B, gated on one shared empirical measurement. E33 is HELD pending that verdict, not re-dispatched yet. (E34's lean re-dispatch already running is internally valid regardless — its instrument is agreement-vs-auditor and cost is a multiplier — but its handoff claim inherits the same equivalence caveat until certified.)
